proof_stages.label: Symmetry-class criterion for GSE statistics
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls that spectral statistics of chaotic quantum systems are selected by unitary and antiunitary symmetries, with GSE arising when the relevant antiunitary time-reversal symmetry squares to -1, conventionally through spin and quaternion-real Hamiltonian blocks.

proof_stages.label: Quantum-graph framework and standard boundary conditions
proof_stages.summary: Quantum graphs are introduced as metric graphs with one-dimensional Schrödinger dynamics on bonds and self-adjoint vertex conditions, and large well-connected graphs are asserted to follow random-matrix statistics according to their symmetries.

proof_stages.label: Direct spinless phase-graph construction of an antiunitary square root of -1
proof_stages.summary: By replacing selected two-valent vertex conditions with phase jumps and combining parity, complex conjugation, and a sign change on one half of a doubled graph, the speaker constructs a spinless quantum graph with an antiunitary symmetry squaring to -1 and hence predicted GSE statistics in the large-graph limit.

proof_stages.label: Representation-theoretic decomposition under geometric symmetries
proof_stages.summary: For systems with a geometric symmetry group, the quantum symmetry operators form a representation, and simultaneous diagonalization/block diagonalization decomposes the spectrum into subspectra associated with irreducible representations, including energy-degenerate multiplets inside each block.

proof_stages.label: Transferred time reversal for pseudo-real representations
proof_stages.summary: The irreducible representations are classified as complex, real, or pseudo-real, and the key table shows that a system with ordinary time reversal squaring to +1 has GSE statistics on a pseudo-real subspectrum because the effective transferred time-reversal operator squares to -1 there.

proof_stages.label: Quaternion-group Cayley graph realizing a pseudo-real GSE subspectrum
proof_stages.summary: The quaternion group is chosen as the simplest group with a pseudo-real representation, its Cayley graph is built with generator-dependent bond lengths and left-multiplication symmetry, and the graph is made sufficiently complex by blowing up vertices into identical subgraphs to obtain a GSE subspectrum.

proof_stages.label: Fundamental-domain isolation and scalar two-copy realization
proof_stages.summary: The pseudo-real subspectrum is isolated by passing to a fundamental domain with representation-induced boundary matrices, then the two-component wavefunction is represented on two graph copies to recover the phase-decorated scalar graph, with final numerical spectra shown close to the GSE curve.

examples.note: A real Hamiltonian is used to make concrete the standard antiunitary symmetry given by complex conjugation, which squares to +1 and leads to the GOE rather than the GSE.
